title: Testing solutions to inequalities (basic)
description: Practice plugging in values to inequalities, like x>5, and checking to see if the inequalities hold true.
topic_path: Get ready for expressions, equations, & inequalities

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which  of the $x$-values are solutions to the following inequality?**



$x\lt 21$



[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's substitute $x=\blueD{3}$  and see if the inequality is true.

$\begin{align} x&\lt 21 \\
\blueD{3}&\stackrel{\large?}{\lt} 21\\
\blueD{3} &\lt  21 \end{align}$

Yes, $x = \blueD{3}$ is a solution!

Let's try $x = \greenD{21}$.

$\begin{align} x&\lt 21 \\
\greenD{21}&\stackrel{\large?}{\lt} 21\\
\greenD{21} &\nless 21 \end{align}$

No, $x = \greenD{21}$ is not a solution.

Now let's try $x = \goldD{200}$.

$\begin{align} x&\lt 21 \\
\goldD{200}&\stackrel{\large?}{\lt} 21\\
\goldD{200} &\nless  21 \end{align}$

No, $x = \goldD{200}$ is not a solution.

The following $x$-value satisfies the inequality $x\lt 21$ :


- $x = \blueD3$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which  of the $x$-values are solutions to both of the following inequalities?**




$19< x$ and $x<25$



[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's substitute $x=\blueD{18}$ and see if the inequalities are true.

$\begin{align} 19&\lt x\\
19&\stackrel{\large?}{\lt} \blueD{18}\\
19 &\nless  \blueD{18}\end{align}$

Since $19< \blueD{18}$ is not true, $x = \blueD{18}$ is not a solution.

Now let's try $x = \greenD{20}$.

$\begin{align} 19&\lt x\\
19&\stackrel{\large?}{\lt} \greenD{20}\\
19 &<  \greenD{20}\end{align}$

Yes, $x = \greenD{20}$ is a solution to  $19< x$.  Let's see if it is also a solution to $x<25$.


$\begin{align} x&\lt 25\\
\greenD{20}&\stackrel{\large?}{\lt} 25\\
\greenD{20} &< 25\end{align}$

Yes, $x = \greenD{20}$ is also a solution to  $x<25$. 

Since $19< \greenD{20}$ and $\greenD{20}<25$ are both true, $x = \greenD{20}$ is a solution!

Let's try $x = \goldD{27}$.

$\begin{align} 19&\lt x\\
19&\stackrel{\large?}{\lt} \goldD{27}\\
19 &<  \goldD{27}\end{align}$

Yes, $x = \goldD{27}$ is a solution to  $19< x$.  Let's see if it is also a solution to $x<25$.


$\begin{align} x&\lt 25\\
\goldD{27}&\stackrel{\large?}{\lt} 25\\
\goldD{27} &\nless 25\end{align}$

No, $x = \goldD{27}$ is not a solution to  $x<25$. 

Since $19< \goldD{27}$ and $\goldD{27}<25$ are not both true, $x = \goldD{27}$ is not a solution to both inequalities.

The following $x$-value satisfies both $19<x$ and $x<25$:


- $x = \greenD{20}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which  of the $x$-values are solutions to both of the following inequalities?**




$4< x$ and $x<40$



[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's substitute $x=\blueD{7}$ and see if the inequalities are true.

$\begin{align} 4&< x\\
4&\stackrel{\large?}{<} \blueD{7}\\
4 &<  \blueD{7}\end{align}$

Yes, $x=\blueD7$ is a solution to $4< x$. Let's try and see if it is also a solution to $x < 40$.

$\begin{align} x&< 40\\
\blueD{7}&\stackrel{\large?}{<} 40\\
\blueD{7} &<  40\end{align}$


Since $4< \blueD{7}$ and $\blueD7<40$ are both true, $x = \blueD{7}$ is a solution!

Now let's try $x = \greenD{40}$.

$\begin{align} 4&< x\\
4&\stackrel{\large?}{<} \greenD{40}\\
4 &<  \greenD{40}\end{align}$

Yes, $x = \greenD{40}$ is a solution to $4<x$. Let's try and see if it is also a solution to $x <40$.

$\begin{align} x&< 40\\
\greenD{40}&\stackrel{\large?}{<} 40\\
\greenD{40} &\nless 40\end{align}$

Since $\greenD{40}<40$ is not true, $x = \greenD{40}$ is not a solution.

Let's try $x = \goldD{51}$.

$\begin{align} 4&< x\\
4&\stackrel{\large?}{<} \goldD{51}\\
4 &<  \goldD{51}\end{align}$

Yes, $x = \goldD{51}$ is a solution to $4< x$. Let's try and see if it is also a solution to $x < 40$.

$\begin{align} x&< 40\\
\goldD{51}&\stackrel{\large?}{<} 40\\
\goldD{51} &\nless  40\end{align}$

Since $\goldD{51}<40$ is not true, $x = \goldD{51}$ is not a solution.

The following $x$-values satisfy both $4< x$ and $x<40$:


- $x = \blueD{7}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which  of the $x$-values are solutions to both of the following inequalities?**



$128\gt x$ and $x\gt 82$



[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's substitute $x=\blueD{83}$ and see if the inequalities are true.

$\begin{align}
128&>x \\
128&\stackrel{\large?}>\blueD{83} \\
128&>\blueD{83}
\end{align}$

Yes, $x=\blueD{83}$ is a solution to $128\gt x$. Let's try and see if it is also a solution to $x \gt 82$.

$\begin{align}
x&>82 \\
\blueD{83}&\stackrel{\large?}>82 \\
\blueD{83}&>82
\end{align}$


Since $128 \gt \blueD{83}$ and $\blueD{83} \gt 82$ are both true, $x = \blueD{83}$ is a solution!

Now let's try $x = \greenD{118}$.

$\begin{align}
128&>x \\
128&\stackrel{\large?}>\greenD{118} \\
128&>\greenD{118}
\end{align}$

Yes, $x=\greenD{118}$ is a solution to $128\gt x$. Let's try and see if it is also a solution to $x \gt 82$.

$\begin{align}
x&>82 \\
\greenD{118}&\stackrel{\large?}>82 \\
\greenD{118}&>82
\end{align}$

Since $128\gt \greenD{118}$ and $\greenD{118}\gt 82$ are both true, $x = \greenD{118}$ is a solution!

Let's try $x = \goldD{127}$.

$\begin{align}
128&>x \\
128&\stackrel{\large?}>\goldD{127} \\
128&>\goldD{127}
\end{align}$

Yes, $x=\goldD{127}$ is a solution to $128\gt x$. Let's try and see if it is also a solution to $x \gt 82$.

$\begin{align}
x&>82 \\
\goldD{127}&\stackrel{\large?}>82 \\
\goldD{127}&>82
\end{align}$


Since $128\gt \goldD{127}$ and $\goldD{127}\gt 82$ are both true, $x = \goldD{127}$ is a solution!

The following $x$-values satisfy both $128\gt x$ and $x\gt 82$
:


- $x = \blueD{83}$
- $x = \greenD{118}$
- $x = \goldD{127}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which  of the $x$-values are solutions to both of the following inequalities?**




$60\gt x$ and $x\gt 57$



[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's substitute $x=\blueD{61}$ and see if the inequalities are true.

$\begin{align}
60&>x \\
60&\stackrel{\large?}>\blueD{61} \\
60&\ngtr\blueD{61}
\end{align}$


Since $60\gt \blueD{61}$ is not true, $x = \blueD{61}$ is not a solution.

Now let's try $x = \greenD{59}$.

$\begin{align}
60&>x \\
60&\stackrel{\large?}> \greenD{59} \\
60&> \greenD{59}
\end{align}$

Yes, $x = \greenD{59}$ is a solution to $60\gt x$. Let's try and see if it is also a solution to $x \gt 57$.

$\begin{align}
x&>57 \\
 \greenD{59}&\stackrel{\large?}>57 \\
\greenD{59}&>57
\end{align}$

Since $60\gt \greenD{59}$ and $\greenD{59}\gt 57$ are both true, $x = \greenD{59}$ is a solution!

Let's try $x = \goldD{50}$.

$\begin{align}
60&>x \\
60&\stackrel{\large?}> \goldD{50} \\
60&> \goldD{50}
\end{align}$

Yes, $x =\goldD{50}$ is a solution to $60\gt x$. Let's try and see if it is also a solution to $x \gt 57$.

$\begin{align}
x&>57 \\
 \goldD{50}&\stackrel{\large?}>57 \\
\goldD{50}&\ngtr 57
\end{align}$


Since $\goldD{50}\gt 57$ is not true, $x = \goldD{50}$ is not a solution.

The following $x$-value satisfies both $60\gt x$ and $x\gt 57$:


- $x = \greenD{59}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which  of the $x$-values are solutions to both of the following inequalities?**




$1\lt x$ and $x\lt13$



[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's substitute $x=\blueD{0}$ and see if the inequalities are true.

$\begin{align}
1&<x \\
1&\stackrel{\large?}<\blueD{0} \\
1&\nless \blueD{0}
\end{align}$


Since $1\lt \blueD0$ is not true, $x = \blueD{0}$ is not a solution.

Now let's try $x = \greenD{2}$.

$\begin{align}
1&<x \\
1&\stackrel{\large?}<\greenD{2} \\
1&< \greenD{2}
\end{align}$

Yes, $x = \greenD{2}$ is a solution to $1\lt x$. Let's try and see if it is also a solution to $x \lt 13$.

$\begin{align}
x&<13 \\
\greenD{2} &\stackrel{\large?}<13 \\
\greenD{2} &< 13
\end{align}$



Since $1\lt \greenD2$ and $\greenD2\lt 13$ are both true, $x = \greenD{2}$ is a solution!

Let's try $x = \goldD{14}$.

$\begin{align}
1&<x \\
1&\stackrel{\large?}< \goldD{14} \\
1&< \goldD{14}
\end{align}$

Yes, $x =\goldD{14}$ is a solution to $1\lt x$. Let's try and see if it is also a solution to $x \lt 13$.

$\begin{align}
x&<13 \\
\goldD{14}&\stackrel{\large?}<13 \\
\goldD{14}&\nless 13
\end{align}$


Since $\goldD{14} \lt 13$ is not true, $x = \goldD{14}$ is not a solution.

The following $x$-value satisfies both $1\lt x$ and $x\lt13$:

- $x = \greenD{2}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which  of the $x$-values are solutions to both of the following inequalities?**




$100\lt x$ and $ x\lt150$



[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's substitute $x=\blueD{180}$ and see if the inequalities are true.

$\begin{align}
100&<x \\
100&\stackrel{\large?}<\blueD{180} \\
100&<\blueD{180}
\end{align}$

Yes, $x=\blueD{180}$ is a solution to $100\lt x$. Let's try and see if it is also a solution to $x \lt 150$.

$\begin{align} x&< 150\\
\blueD{180}&\stackrel{\large?}{<} 150\\
\blueD{180} &\nless  150\end{align}$



Since $\blueD{180}\lt 150$ is not true, $x = \blueD{180}$ is not a solution.

Now let's try $x = \greenD{130}$.

$\begin{align}
100&<x \\
100&\stackrel{\large?}<\greenD{130} \\
100&<\greenD{130}
\end{align}$

Yes, $x=\greenD{130}$ is a solution to $100\lt x$. Let's try and see if it is also a solution to $x \lt 150$.

$\begin{align} x&< 150\\
\greenD{130}&\stackrel{\large?}{<} 150\\
\greenD{130} &<  150\end{align}$


Since $100\lt \greenD{130}$ and $\greenD{130}\lt 150$ are both true, $x = \greenD{130}$ is a solution!

Let's try $x = \goldD{101}$.

$\begin{align}
100&<x \\
100&\stackrel{\large?}<\goldD{101} \\
100&<\goldD{101}
\end{align}$

Yes, $x=\goldD{101}$ is a solution to $100\lt x$. Let's try and see if it is also a solution to $x \lt 150$.

$\begin{align} x&< 150\\
\goldD{101}&\stackrel{\large?}{<} 150\\
\goldD{101} &<  150\end{align}$


Since $100\lt\goldD{101}$ and $\goldD{101} \lt 150$ are both true, $x = \goldD{101}$ is a solution!

The following $x$-values satisfy both $100\lt x$ and $ x\lt150$:


- $x = \greenD{130}$
- $x = \goldD{101}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which  of the $x$-values are solutions to both of the following inequalities?**




$30> x$ and $ x> 15$



[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's substitute $x=\blueD{15}$ and see if the inequalities are true.

$\begin{align} 
30&> x\\
30&\stackrel{\large?}{>} \blueD{15}\\
30 &>  \blueD{15}\end{align}$

Yes, $x=\blueD{15}$ is a solution to $30> x$. Let's try and see if it is also a solution to $x >15$.

$\begin{align} 
x&> 15\\
\blueD{15}&\stackrel{\large?}{>} 15\\
\blueD{15} &\ngtr  15\end{align}$


Since $\blueD{15}>15$ is not true, $x = \blueD{15}$ is not a solution.

Now let's try $x = \greenD{22}$.

$\begin{align} 
30&> x\\
30&\stackrel{\large?}{>} \greenD{22}\\
30 &>  \greenD{22}\end{align}$

Yes, $x=\greenD{22}$ is a solution to $30> x$. Let's try and see if it is also a solution to $x > 15$.

$\begin{align} 
x&> 15\\
\greenD{22}&\stackrel{\large?}{>} 15\\
\greenD{22} &>  15\end{align}$


Since $30> \greenD{22}$ and $\greenD{22}>15$ are both true, $x = \greenD{22}$ is a solution!

Let's try $x = \goldD{29}$.

$\begin{align} 
30&> x\\
30&\stackrel{\large?}{>} \goldD{29}\\
30 &>  \goldD{29}\end{align}$

Yes, $x=\goldD{29}$ is a solution to $30> x$. Let's try and see if it is also a solution to $x > 15$.

$\begin{align} 
x&> 15\\
\goldD{29}&\stackrel{\large?}{>} 15\\
\goldD{29} &>  15\end{align}$


Since $30> \goldD{29}$ and $\goldD{29}>15$ are both true, $x = \goldD{29}$ is a solution!

The following $x$-values satisfy both $30> x$ and $ x> 15$:


- $x = \greenD{22}$
- $x = \goldD{29}$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which  of the $x$-values are solutions to both of the following inequalities?**




$81> x$ and $ x> 68$



[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's substitute $x=\blueD{88}$ and see if the inequalities are true.

$\begin{align}
81&> x \\
81&\stackrel{\large?} > \blueD{88} \\
81&\ngtr\blueD{88}
\end{align}$


Since $81> \blueD{88}$ is not true, $x = \blueD{88}$ is not a solution.
Now let's try $x = \greenD{85}$.

$\begin{align}
81&> x \\
81&\stackrel{\large?} > \greenD{85} \\
81&\ngtr\greenD{85}
\end{align}$


Since $81> \greenD{85}$ is not true, $x = \greenD{85}$ is not a solution.
Let's try $x = \goldD{80}$.

$\begin{align} 
81&> x\\
81&\stackrel{\large?}{>} \goldD{80}\\
81 &>  \goldD{80}\end{align}$

Yes, $x=\goldD{80}$ is a solution to $81> x$. Let's try and see if it is also a solution to $x > 68$.

$\begin{align} 
x&> 68\\
\goldD{80}&\stackrel{\large?}{>} 68\\
\goldD{80} &>  68\end{align}$


Since $81> \goldD{80}$ and $\goldD{80}>68$ are both true, $x = \goldD{80}$ is a solution!
The following $x$-value satisfies both $81> x$ and $ x> 68$:


- $x = \goldD{80}$

The values that make the inequality true are  $\blueD{-1.2}$, $\goldD{-0.1}$, and $\green{0.8}$.
